originContent: We have a discord, please join and direct any questions there. Myself or someone else will happily answer them.
translatedContent: We have a discord, please join and direct any questions there. Myself or someone else will happily answer them.

originContent: the Rexglue SDK discord for helping with any info they have
translatedContent: the Rexglue SDK discord for helping with any info they have

originContent: SolarCookies for midasm hooks and future use of CRT functions and the reNut Launcher
translatedContent: SolarCookies for midasm hooks and future use of CRT functions and the reNut Launcher

originContent: US version of banjo-kazooie: nuts and bolts default.xex
translatedContent: US version of banjo-kazooie: nuts and bolts default.xex

originContent: NOTE: YOU MUST DELETE ALL INSTANCES OF .gitignore OTHERWISE WHATEVER YOU COMPILE WITH WONT SEE THE FILES IN THE AREAS THEY ARE IN.
translatedContent: NOTE: YOU MUST DELETE ALL INSTANCES OF .gitignore OTHERWISE WHATEVER YOU COMPILE WITH WONT SEE THE FILES IN THE AREAS THEY ARE IN.

originContent: NOTE: YOU MUST INSTALL GIT BEFORE INSTALLING THE REXGLUE-SDK, OR BUILDING THIS REPO.
translatedContent: NOTE: YOU MUST INSTALL GIT BEFORE INSTALLING THE REXGLUE-SDK, OR BUILDING THIS REPO.

originContent: NOTE: NOTE: YOU MUST BUILD AND INSTALL THE MAIN REPO OF Rexglue-SDK BEFORE CONTINUING
translatedContent: NOTE: NOTE: YOU MUST BUILD AND INSTALL THE MAIN REPO OF Rexglue-SDK BEFORE CONTINUING

originContent: 2. Inside the assets folder you need to extract your banjo-kazooie: Nuts&Bolts iso's contents and the default.xex. I reccomend using iso extract. (I don't know what linux users use. I use Windows, however iso extract does work on linux through WINE.)
translatedContent: 2. Inside the assets folder you need to extract your banjo-kazooie: Nuts&Bolts iso's contents and the default.xex. I reccomend using iso extract. (I don't know what linux users use. I use Windows, however iso extract does work on linux through WINE.)

originContent: 3. Inside your cloned git open a terminal and run ```rexglue migrate --app_root .``` This ensures that if anything with codegen changes on Rexglue, you can codegen properly.
translatedContent: 3. Inside your cloned git open a terminal and run ```rexglue migrate --app_root .``` This ensures that if anything with codegen changes on Rexglue, you can codegen properly.

originContent: 3b. You must then either delete your out folder, or if your on Windows you can open VS, right click your cmakelists.txt, and delete cache and reconfigure so you codegen with the version your using.
translatedContent: 3b. You must then either delete your out folder, or if your on Windows you can open VS, right click your cmakelists.txt, and delete cache and reconfigure so you codegen with the version your using.

originContent: 4. You can now open a terminal and run ```rexglue codegen renut_config.toml``` which will give you the ppc files to recompile in the generated folder.
translatedContent: 4. You can now open a terminal and run ```rexglue codegen renut_config.toml``` which will give you the ppc files to recompile in the generated folder.

originContent: 5. If you're on Windows, you can open the project in VS, change the build type to `win-amd64-relwithdebinfo`, then build all.
translatedContent: 5. If you're on Windows, you can open the project in VS, change the build type to `win-amd64-relwithdebinfo`, then build all.

originContent: 5b. If you're on Linux, and don't have access to VS, you will need to use a terminal and run ```cmake --preset linux-amd64-relwithdebinfo``` and then ```cmake --build --preset linux-amd64-relwithdebinfo```. (You can do this on Windows as well, just replace `linux` with `windows`.)
translatedContent: 5b. If you're on Linux, and don't have access to VS, you will need to use a terminal and run ```cmake --preset linux-amd64-relwithdebinfo``` and then ```cmake --build --preset linux-amd64-relwithdebinfo```. (You can do this on Windows as well, just replace `linux` with `windows`.)

originContent: 6. Once its compiled, you need to have the built exe in the same directory as the assets, otherwise the game won't open.
translatedContent: 6. Once its compiled, you need to have the built exe in the same directory as the assets, otherwise the game won't open.

originContent: if you wish to build for linux please use this fork of reNut made by etonedemid it requires using his fork of rexlgue and he has made a pr to rexglue so this stuff becomes part of main.
translatedContent: if you wish to build for linux please use this fork of reNut made by etonedemid it requires using his fork of rexlgue and he has made a pr to rexglue so this stuff becomes part of main.

originContent: IF YOU DONT WANT TO BUILD
translatedContent: IF YOU DONT WANT TO BUILD

originContent: Building is mainly for those who either would rather build or want to help develop the game with myself and others. If you don't want to build;
translatedContent: Building is mainly for those who either would rather build or want to help develop the game with myself and others. If you don't want to build;

originContent: * Go to https://goopie.xyz/ and download the goopie launcher
translatedContent: * Go to https://goopie.xyz/ and download the goopie launcher

originContent: * In the launcher select banjo-kazooie: nuts and bolts
translatedContent: * In the launcher select banjo-kazooie: nuts and bolts

originContent: * Click Select ISO and select your iso for banjo-kazooie: nuts and bolts (must be the north american release) and wait for extraction to finish
translatedContent: * Click Select ISO and select your iso for banjo-kazooie: nuts and bolts (must be the north american release) and wait for extraction to finish

originContent: * After extraction is finished click the update button, the launcher will then download the latest release of the windows version of renut
translatedContent: * After extraction is finished click the update button, the launcher will then download the latest release of the windows version of renut

originContent: 1. Animations are a little jank (there's jitter, banjo's and others bones break, some of the animations are half done, and some other little ancillaries) but they do not inhibit gameplay, it's just funny to see happen.
translatedContent: 1. Animations are a little jank (there's jitter, banjo's and others bones break, some of the animations are half done, and some other little ancillaries) but they do not inhibit gameplay, it's just funny to see happen.

originContent: The Issues tab is a place for things like crashes that happen in game that arent already noted, please refrain from making issues like "Game wont open" or "Have to use ISOs?".
translatedContent: The Issues tab is a place for things like crashes that happen in game that arent already noted, please refrain from making issues like "Game wont open" or "Have to use ISOs?".

originContent: You must use the crash template for any issues that pertain to crashes (I'll probably make other templates), for I don't need it flooded with mostly user error posts. Please join the discord and use the #help channel if you have an issue unrelated to a crash.
translatedContent: You must use the crash template for any issues that pertain to crashes (I'll probably make other templates), for I don't need it flooded with mostly user error posts. Please join the discord and use the #help channel if you have an issue unrelated to a crash.
